title: Compare five aminoglycoside biosynthetic gene clusters
summary: Run LOSATP in the browser, assign CDS-derived proteins from five biosynthetic gene clusters to gbdraw similarity groups, apply antiSMASH gene_kind color rules, and label the first record.
primaryWorkflow: LOSATP similarity groups (Orthogroups mode) with feature color rules
requirements: GenBank features that retain antiSMASH qualifiers such as gene_kind.

downloads.label: BGC0000708
downloads.note: Download the lividomycin biosynthetic gene cluster as GenBank.

downloads.label: BGC0000709
downloads.note: Download the neomycin biosynthetic gene cluster as GenBank.

downloads.label: BGC0000711
downloads.note: Download the second neomycin biosynthetic gene cluster as GenBank.

downloads.label: BGC0000712
downloads.note: Download the paromomycin biosynthetic gene cluster as GenBank.

downloads.label: BGC0000713
downloads.note: Download the ribostamycin biosynthetic gene cluster as GenBank.

downloads.label: Default color table
downloads.note: Sets the fallback CDS color used by the runnable command.

downloads.label: Specific color table
downloads.note: Contains the four antiSMASH gene_kind rules used in the figure.

downloads.label: Qualifier priority table
downloads.note: Uses the gene qualifier for concise CDS labels.

manualSteps.title: Switch to Linear mode
manualSteps.body: Open gbdraw.app and select Linear mode before adding inputs.

manualSteps.title: Upload the five GenBank files
manualSteps.body: Add the GenBank files in this order, then fill the Organism / strain and Subtitle / title fields exactly as shown. Type the <i> tags literally; the generated SVG uses them to italicize taxon names.

media.alt: Linear GenBank row 1 with BGC0000708.gbk and its Organism / strain and Subtitle / title values.
media.caption: Enter the row 1 values from the table, including the literal Streptomyces lividus markup, then repeat the remaining rows in order.

manualSteps.title: Configure browser LOSAT
manualSteps.body: In Pairwise Comparisons, choose Run LOSAT. Use LOSATP with blastp mode Orthogroups.
manualSteps.note: This example compares CDS-derived proteins across related biosynthetic gene clusters. The Orthogroups mode uses retained protein-search hits to form gbdraw similarity groups across all five records and draws links between group members; it does not infer phylogeny-based orthogroups.

media.alt: Run LOSAT settings with LOSATP Orthogroups, Auto execution, Safe total threads, and the 50, 1e-2, 30, and 0 thresholds.
media.caption: Set LOSATP Orthogroups with Auto execution, Safe total threads, and thresholds 50 / 1e-2 / 30 / 0.

media.alt: LOSAT Settings panel with the blastp mode menu open and the Orthogroups option highlighted.
media.caption: Open blastp mode in LOSAT Settings and choose the highlighted Orthogroups option.

manualSteps.title: Set record orientation and comparison layout
manualSteps.body: Reverse-complement BGC0000713, keep features in the middle of each axis, and draw curved pairwise matches before the first generation.

media.alt: Fifth Linear input row for BGC0000713.gbk with the Region reverse complement checkbox checked.
media.caption: Use the BGC0000713 row's Region controls and check Reverse complement for that record only.

media.alt: Linear Layout card with the Track Layout menu open and the Middle option highlighted.
media.caption: Open Track Layout in the Layout card and choose the highlighted Middle option.

media.alt: Pairwise Match card with the Pairwise Match Style menu open and the Curve option highlighted.
media.caption: Open Pairwise Match Style in the Pairwise Match card and choose the highlighted Curve option.

manualSteps.title: Set labels, colors, and figure layout
manualSteps.body: Show labels on the first record, use gene for CDS label text, rotate labels by 45 degrees, and choose the orange palette. Set unmatched CDS features to #dddddd; the table lists the remaining layout values.

media.alt: Labels card with the Show Labels menu open and First Record Only highlighted.
media.caption: Open Show Labels and choose First Record Only; keep the CDS qualifier priority set to gene as listed in the table.

media.alt: Axis and Scale card with the Scale Style menu open and the Ruler option highlighted.
media.caption: Open Scale Style in the Axis & Scale card and choose the highlighted Ruler option.

media.alt: Title and Legend card with the Legend Position menu open and the Bottom option highlighted.
media.caption: Open Legend Position in the Title & Legend card and choose the highlighted Bottom option.

manualSteps.title: Add the plot title and record text
manualSteps.body: Copy the Plot Title from the table, including the literal Streptomyces markup, and keep Plot Title Position at Bottom (Default).

rows: Aminoglycoside biosynthetic gene clusters from Streptomyces spp.

media.alt: Compact Title and Legend controls with the Plot Title input, Bottom position, and linear definition-text settings.
media.caption: Use the Plot Title field for the title in the table and keep the listed position and definition-text settings.

actions.script: const card = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.card')).find((node) => node.querySelector('summary')?.textContent?.includes('Title & Legend')); const content = card?.querySelector('details > div'); const controls = Array.from(content?.children || []).slice(0, 3); if (controls.length === 0) throw new Error('BGC title controls not found.'); controls.forEach((node) => node.setAttribute('data-capture', 'bgc-title-controls')); controls[0].scrollIntoView({ block: 'center' });

manualSteps.title: Add feature color rules
manualSteps.body: After choosing the orange palette and setting the default CDS color to #dddddd, create four CDS rules from the antiSMASH gene_kind qualifier before generating the final figure.
manualSteps.note: Use biosynthetic$ for the core category so it does not also catch biosynthetic-additional.

media.alt: SPECIFIC RULES (-t) controls showing four CDS gene_kind rules for biosynthetic$, biosynthetic-additional, transport, and regulatory categories.
media.caption: Enter all four antiSMASH gene_kind rules in the SPECIFIC RULES (-t) controls before clicking Generate Diagram.

actions.script: const app = window.__GBDRAW_APP__; if (app) app.sidebarWidth = 420; const heading = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('h4')).find((node) => node.textContent?.includes('SPECIFIC RULES')); const header = heading?.parentElement; const rows = header?.nextElementSibling; if (!header || !rows) throw new Error('BGC specific-rules rows not found.'); header.setAttribute('data-capture', 'bgc-specific-rules-header'); rows.setAttribute('data-capture', 'bgc-specific-rules-rows'); rows.scrollIntoView({ block: 'center' });

manualSteps.title: Generate and align to og_1
manualSteps.body: Click Generate Diagram to create the initial comparison. In the preview, click the livE CDS assigned to og_1 and choose Align; the web app regenerates the diagram with the cached LOSATP results.

media.alt: The livE feature assigned to og_1 beside its feature popup with Align available.
media.caption: Click the livE CDS in og_1, then choose Align to align the records on that group.

actions.script: document.querySelector('.preview-feature-search')?.style.setProperty('display', 'none', 'important'); const app = window.__GBDRAW_APP__; if (app) { app.resultPanelTab = 'preview'; app.zoom = 1.35; if (app.svgContainer) app.svgContainer.style.transform = 'translate(-130px, -40px) scale(1.35)'; }

const app = window.__GBDRAW_APP__; const target = document.querySelector("[data-gbdraw-feature-id='fed46a3a6']"); const rect = target?.getBoundingClientRect(); if (app?.clickedFeature) app.clickedFeature.activeTab = 'edit'; if (app?.clickedFeaturePos && rect) { app.clickedFeaturePos.x = Math.min(window.innerWidth - 744, rect.right + 32); app.clickedFeaturePos.y = Math.max(24, rect.top - 80); }

() => { const app = window.__GBDRAW_APP__; const text = document.querySelector('.feature-popup')?.textContent || ''; return app?.clickedFeature?.svg_id === 'fed46a3a6' && text.includes('livE') && text.includes('Align'); }

const popup = document.querySelector('.feature-popup'); const body = popup?.querySelector('.feature-popup-body'); const actions = body?.querySelector('.feature-popup-grid > .space-y-3 > .grid.grid-cols-2.gap-2'); if (!popup || !actions) throw new Error('Compact Align popup controls not found.'); popup.children[0]?.setAttribute('data-capture', 'align-popup-header'); popup.children[1]?.setAttribute('data-capture', 'align-popup-tabs'); actions.setAttribute('data-capture', 'align-popup-actions');

media.alt: Final og_1-aligned aminoglycoside BGC comparison with five rows, curved similarity-group matches, rotated first-record labels, and a bottom legend.
media.caption: Check the final og_1-aligned result for five BGC rows, curved matches, rotated first-record labels, and the bottom legend.

manualSteps.title: Inspect a similarity-group ribbon
manualSteps.body: Click a pairwise match ribbon to inspect the gbdraw group ID, member count, record coverage, and member table.

media.alt: Selected og_18 pairwise ribbon beside the similarity-group popup for livA.
media.caption: Click the og_18 ribbon; the popup shows livA, 6 members, coverage across 5 records, and the member table.

actions.script: document.querySelector('.preview-feature-search')?.style.setProperty('display', 'none', 'important'); const app = window.__GBDRAW_APP__; if (app) { app.resultPanelTab = 'preview'; app.zoom = 1.4; if (app.svgContainer) app.svgContainer.style.transform = 'translate(-180px, -40px) scale(1.4)'; }
const app = window.__GBDRAW_APP__; if (app?.clickedPairwiseMatchPos) { app.clickedPairwiseMatchPos.x = 520; app.clickedPairwiseMatchPos.y = 280; }

manualSteps.title: Inspect the feature qualifiers
manualSteps.body: Click the livE CDS and open Qualifiers to inspect the gene and gene_kind values. In this example, gene supplies the livE label and gene_kind=biosynthetic matches the core biosynthetic color rule.

media.alt: Selected livE CDS beside the Qualifiers tab showing gene and gene_kind metadata.
media.caption: For livE, gene supplies the label and gene_kind=biosynthetic explains the core biosynthetic color.

actions.script: document.querySelector('.preview-feature-search')?.style.setProperty('display', 'none', 'important'); const app = window.__GBDRAW_APP__; if (app) { app.resultPanelTab = 'preview'; app.zoom = 1.35; if (app.svgContainer) app.svgContainer.style.transform = 'translate(-130px, -40px) scale(1.35)'; }

const app = window.__GBDRAW_APP__; const target = document.querySelector("[data-gbdraw-feature-id='fed46a3a6']"); const rect = target?.getBoundingClientRect(); if (app?.clickedFeature) app.clickedFeature.activeTab = 'qualifiers'; if (app?.clickedFeaturePos && rect) { app.clickedFeaturePos.x = Math.min(window.innerWidth - 744, rect.right + 32); app.clickedFeaturePos.y = Math.max(24, rect.top - 80); }

() => { const app = window.__GBDRAW_APP__; const feature = app?.clickedFeature; const text = document.querySelector('.feature-popup')?.textContent || ''; return feature?.svg_id === 'fed46a3a6' && feature?.feat?.gene === 'livE' && feature?.feat?.qualifiers?.gene_kind?.includes('biosynthetic') && text.includes('gene_kind'); }

const popup = document.querySelector('.feature-popup'); const target = Array.from(popup?.querySelectorAll('*') || []).find((node) => node.children.length === 0 && node.textContent?.trim() === 'gene_kind'); target?.scrollIntoView({ block: 'center' });

const popup = document.querySelector('.feature-popup'); const leaf = (key) => Array.from(popup?.querySelectorAll('*') || []).find((node) => node.children.length === 0 && node.textContent?.trim() === key); const geneRow = leaf('gene')?.closest('.border-b'); const geneKindRow = leaf('gene_kind')?.closest('.border-b'); if (!popup || !geneRow || !geneKindRow) throw new Error('Compact qualifier rows not found.'); popup.children[0]?.setAttribute('data-capture', 'qualifier-popup-header'); popup.children[1]?.setAttribute('data-capture', 'qualifier-popup-tabs'); geneRow.setAttribute('data-capture', 'qualifier-gene-row'); geneKindRow.setAttribute('data-capture', 'qualifier-gene-kind-row');

colorRules.title: Use gene_kind for antiSMASH categories
colorRules.body: These BGC records carry antiSMASH gene_kind qualifiers for core biosynthetic, additional biosynthetic, transport, and regulatory categories. Use those annotations instead of matching broad product terms.

colorRules.title: Use gene for label text
colorRules.body: Set the CDS qualifier priority to gene so rotated labels use concise gene names when available.

colorRules.title: Keep regex values specific
colorRules.body: Specific rule values are matching patterns. Use biosynthetic$ for core biosynthetic genes and a separate biosynthetic-additional rule for the additional category.

colorRules.title: Use product only as a fallback
colorRules.body: When gene_kind is absent, product can group functional categories, but terms such as protein, enzyme, or transporter may match more features than intended.

colorRules.title: Use descriptive legend captions
colorRules.body: Use captions such as Core biosynthetic genes and Transport-related genes instead of raw qualifier values.

losatTips.title: Start with Auto execution
losatTips.body: Auto uses threaded LOSAT only when the browser supports it and the workload justifies it.

losatTips.title: Prefer Safe total threads
losatTips.body: Safe leaves some logical CPUs unused so the browser can remain responsive.

losatTips.title: Use similarity groups for protein-level BGC comparison
losatTips.body: The Orthogroups mode uses retained protein-search hits to form gbdraw similarity groups even when local gene order differs across clusters. It does not perform phylogeny-based orthology inference.

losatTips.title: Tune the match thresholds together
losatTips.body: Raise the identity, bit score, or minimum-length threshold, or lower the E-value cutoff, when the figure has too many weak ribbons. Relax those thresholds when expected protein matches are missing.

losatTips.title: Reuse the cache
losatTips.body: The app reuses LOSAT results when the input files and LOSAT settings have not changed, so label, color, and legend edits avoid another search.

troubleshooting.title: The rows do not line up like the Gallery figure
troubleshooting.body: Check the input order, the alignment to gbdraw group og_1, and the reverse-complement setting for BGC0000713.

troubleshooting.title: Core genes are not red
troubleshooting.body: Check that the input GenBank files include gene_kind qualifiers and that the core rule uses biosynthetic$.

troubleshooting.title: Labels are missing or too dense
troubleshooting.body: Use Show labels: first record, label placement above feature, and 45° label rotation. If needed, reduce labels by tightening the label filter.

troubleshooting.title: Threaded LOSAT is unavailable
troubleshooting.body: Use Auto or Serial execution. Some browser contexts disable SharedArrayBuffer or cross-origin isolation, which prevents threaded WebAssembly.

troubleshooting.title: The generated diagram differs from the Gallery image
troubleshooting.body: Check the Orthogroups mode, identity, bit score, E-value, and minimum-length thresholds, track layout, legend position, label rotation, color rules, and record orientation.

related.label: Compare with the Hepatoplasmataceae protein-similarity example
